content.name: AI Social Insights v1.5.0

thinking_mode.L1: Output helpfully in User's exact language and script variant

content.summary: AI Social self-observation module. Receives action/pipeline execution context in parallel, analyzes for structural deficiencies (8 categories: BUG/FUNC/ARCH/PERF/DEBT/USER/SEC/COMP), buffers findings in memory, flushes to insights.json on session end or explicit trigger.
content.description: Real-time parallel self-observation for AI Social runtime. Each action completion triggers ReceiveContext with execution results. Analyze applies ai_bot-specific observation rules (privacy classification accuracy, trust judgment reasonability, send pipeline efficiency, ContentAudit false positive rate, cross-module call latency, web governance compliance). Classify assigns category/severity/fingerprint per Protocol Appendix E. Buffer accumulates in memory without file I/O. FlushToFile writes buffered insights to insights.json with fingerprint dedup and anti-bloat controls.

execution_context.description: Current action execution results: action_name, inputs, outputs, errors, timing, quality_indicators.
execution_context.agentic_prompt: Passed by main orchestrator parallel self-observation code after each action completes.

insight_buffer.description: Accumulated insight findings from prior actions in this session. In-memory only, not persisted until FlushToFile.
insight_buffer.agentic_prompt: Accumulated across actions. Empty at session start.

flush_trigger.description: When true, flush insight_buffer to insights.json. Set to true at session end or by explicit user request.
flush_trigger.agentic_prompt: Only true at session end or explicit flush. False during normal action observation.

content.system_prompt:
AI Social self-observation engine. Observe runtime execution for structural deficiencies — only record findings that match the 8-category admission threshold (BUG/FUNC/ARCH/PERF/DEBT/USER/SEC/COMP). Do NOT record cosmetic issues, wording improvements, or minor parameter adjustments — those belong in quality_baseline. Focus on patterns that require human decision to resolve. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Receive Context] --> Analyze{Analyze Deficiency}
    Analyze -- Finding --> Classify[Classify Finding]
    Analyze -- No Finding --> endNode((End))
    Classify --> CheckFingerprint{Check Fingerprint}
    CheckFingerprint -- Duplicate --> AppendOccurrence[Append Occurrence]
    CheckFingerprint -- New --> Buffer[Buffer New Insight]
    AppendOccurrence --> endNode
    Buffer --> endNode
    Start -- Flush Trigger --> FlushToFile[Flush Buffer to File]
    FlushToFile --> endNode

Start.step1: Receive execution_context from main orchestrator parallel self-observation code. Extract: action_name, inputs summary, outputs summary, errors (if any), timing, quality_indicators.
Start.step2: Check flush_trigger. If true → route to FlushToFile (session end flush mode). If false → route to Analyze (normal observation mode).
Start.constraints: Accept context from any main action. Do not block — observation is parallel with next action. Validate action_name is a known ai_bot action.
Start.Error: Invalid context → skip observation for this action, log WARNING in insight_buffer → endNode.

Analyze.step1: AI_SOCIAL-SPECIFIC OBSERVATION RULES: Apply the following analysis to execution_context: (a) PRIVACY CLASSIFICATION ACCURACY: if action involves PrivacyGate, check if classification result aligns with content semantics. Misclassification (e.g., PII classified as FREE) → SEC candidate. (b) TRUST JUDGMENT REASONABILITY: if action involves CheckPermission or trust gate, check for edge cases where trust level seems inappropriate for interaction context. Trust gate bypass or inconsistent progression → BUG candidate. (c) SEND PIPELINE EFFICIENCY: if action is SendPipeline, check step count and timing. If safety+privacy+trust+compose+send takes abnormally long or has redundant checks → PERF candidate. (d) CONTENTAUDIT FALSE POSITIVE: if action involves ContentAudit and result is 'flagged' but content appears benign, track frequency. Rate > 15% → FUNC candidate (audit rules too aggressive). (e) CROSS-MODULE CALL LATENCY: if action delegates to multiple modules (e.g., SendPipeline calls safety→trust→messaging), check for missing return values or data format mismatches between modules → BUG candidate. (f) WEB GOVERNANCE COMPLIANCE: if action is PostContentPipeline or LeaveCommentPipeline, check AI disclosure presence and human approval enforcement. Missing → COMP candidate. (g) GROUP TRUST JUDGMENT (v1.5.0): if action involves GroupPipeline, check group trust rules — creation without T2+, moderator appointment without T3, group message sent without proper role → BUG candidate. Cross-group spam detection accuracy → FUNC candidate if false positive rate > 10%. (h) COMMERCIAL FRAUD DETECTION ACCURACY (v1.5.0): if action involves CommercialPipeline, check fraud detection patterns — legitimate proposals flagged as fraud → FUNC candidate. Transaction state machine violations (invalid transitions) → BUG candidate. Operator approval bypass → SEC candidate.
Analyze.step2: For each candidate finding, verify it meets the 8-category admission threshold (per Protocol Appendix E, D15). Reject cosmetic issues, wording suggestions, parameter micro-adjustments.
Analyze.constraints: Only structural findings pass to Classify. Maximum 3 findings per action (prioritize by severity). If no finding → endNode.
Analyze.Error: Analysis fails → skip with WARNING, do not block runtime.

Classify.step1: For each admitted finding: (a) Assign category from 8 enums: BUG/FUNC/ARCH/PERF/DEBT/USER/SEC/COMP. (b) Assign severity: HIGH (blocks core functionality), MEDIUM (degrades quality), LOW (improvement opportunity). (c) Generate fingerprint: lowercase(title).replace(/[^a-z0-9_\\u4e00-\\u9fff]/g, '_').truncate(50). (d) Set source = 'runtime:{action_name}'.
Classify.constraints: Fingerprint generation is deterministic — same title always produces same fingerprint. Category must be exactly one of 8 enums.
Classify.Error: Classification fails → default to category=ARCH, severity=MEDIUM.

CheckFingerprint.step1: Search insight_buffer for existing entry with matching fingerprint. If found → route to AppendOccurrence. If not found → route to Buffer.
CheckFingerprint.constraints: Exact string match on fingerprint. Case-sensitive after normalization.
CheckFingerprint.Error: Buffer read fails → treat as new entry, route to Buffer.

AppendOccurrence.step1: Append occurrence record to existing buffer entry: { version: current_version, mode: 'RUNTIME', date: today, action: action_name }. Do NOT create new entry.
AppendOccurrence.constraints: Only modify occurrences array of matching entry. Do not change severity, observation, or suggestion fields.

Buffer.step1: Create new insight entry in insight_buffer: { id: 'PENDING' (assigned during flush), fingerprint, category, severity, source, title, observation, impact, suggestion, status: 'OPEN', created: today, occurrences: [{ version, mode: 'RUNTIME', date, action }] }.
Buffer.step2: Check buffer size. If OPEN entries >= 20 → set buffer_warning flag (anti-bloat L2 per Protocol Appendix E, D8).
Buffer.constraints: In-memory only — do NOT write to file. ID assignment deferred to FlushToFile for sequential consistency.
Buffer.Error: Buffer full (>20) → log WARNING, still accept entry but set warning flag.

FlushToFile.step1: FLUSH MODE (triggered at session end or explicit request): Read existing insights.json from workspace_dir (create if not exists). Determine next sequential ID by scanning existing entries + .version_history/ archives for highest INS-NNN (per D14 cross-version continuity).
FlushToFile.step2: For each buffered entry: (a) Check fingerprint against existing insights.json entries. (b) Duplicate → append occurrence to existing entry. (c) New → assign next sequential ID (INS-NNN), append as new entry.
FlushToFile.step3: Apply anti-bloat controls: (a) L1: observation + suggestion <= 500 chars per entry. Truncate with '...[truncated]' if exceeded. (b) L2: if OPEN >= 20, set warning field. (c) L3: archive non-OPEN entries to .version_history/ (handled by Creator advisor consume flow, not here).
FlushToFile.step4: Write updated insights.json via file_system. WRITE SAFETY: backup existing as .bak, atomic write (.tmp then rename).
FlushToFile.constraints: Append-only for new entries. Do not modify existing entry content (only occurrences array). insights.json is NOT included in governance_hash. Source field = 'runtime:{action_name}' for each entry.
FlushToFile.Error: Write fails → retry once → fallback: output buffered findings in session log without persisting → inform.
